unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: Celebrate the learner's first year of English through a supported picture-and-word portfolio, six-page mini-book, one-minute presentation and next-goal reflection.
unit.learningPath:
Choose favourite work.
Review 30 useful words.
Listen to the capstone story and project brief.
Make a six-page picture-and-word book.
Practise and share a one-minute presentation.
Complete the checkpoint and supported reflection.
unit.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 Grade 1 capstone

visual.alt: Young learners sharing picture-and-word English portfolios

readings.title: Amal's English Year
readings.theme: celebrating Grade 1 words, stories, speaking and early writing
readings.passageScript: Amal opened her learning folder. She found letters, family pictures, games, shapes, farm animals, senses, vehicles, water and town places. She chose her favourite work and made a new book called My First English World. On celebration day she pointed, read a few words and said, 'My name is Amal. I can speak English. I like my book.' Everyone clapped.

readings.title: My Capstone Steps
readings.theme: celebrating Grade 1 words, stories, speaking and early writing
readings.passageScript: Choose three favourite pieces. Make a six-page picture-and-word book. Add labels and short patterns. Practise a one-minute show-and-tell. Share it with your teacher and complete your reflection.

readings.title: Celebration Dialogue
readings.theme: celebrating Grade 1 words, stories, speaking and early writing
readings.passageScript: Teacher: What is your favourite page? Learner: I like my family page. Teacher: What can you say? Learner: This is my mum. I love my family. Teacher: What is your next goal? Learner: I will read more words.

grammar.title: Language pattern 1
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: My name is ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: My name is ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 2
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: I can ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 3
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I like ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: I like ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 4
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: This is a ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: This is a ___.

grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: My next goal is ___.

writing.title: My First English World page 1
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw, label and complete the pattern: My name is ___.
writing.expectedLength: One picture, 1-3 labels and one short pattern
writing.successCriteria: The picture and words match; I said the idea first; I used the model; I read or repeated it aloud
writing.support: Tracing, copying, word cards, dictation to an adult and oral recording are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second sentence or question.

assignments.title: My First English World capstone
assignments.instructions: Submit three favourite portfolio pieces, the six-page picture-and-word mini-book, a one-minute presentation or recording, and a supported reflection with one next goal.

quizzes.question: Which is a complete sentence?
quizzes.options: This is a cat. | cat this | a cat | is cat
quizzes.explanation: The correct answer is This is a cat..

quizzes.question: Which word names a family member?
Which action happens in a game?
Which animal lives on a farm?
Which body part helps you hear?
Which sentence tells a safe town action?
quizzes.options: Stop at the red light. | Run into the road. | Drop litter. | Push people.
quizzes.explanation: The correct answer is Stop at the red light..

quizzes.question: What should you do before sharing your capstone?
quizzes.options: Practise and check it. | Hide every page. | Remove all words. | Skip the reflection.
quizzes.explanation: The correct answer is Practise and check it..

liveSessions.title: Launch and choose
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring the capstone folder, crayons and the current page.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello; 5 min model; 12 min capstone work; 5 min share; 3 min celebrate
liveSessions.afterSession: Show the new or improved capstone part to a caregiver.

teacherNotes.note: Adult support may include reading instructions, scribing a dictated sentence and operating the recorder. Keep the learner's own choices, voice, drawing and language visible.
Treat presentation day as a warm celebration, not a high-pressure performance. Accept live, recorded or one-to-one sharing.
